title: Scarlet fever: guidance and data
body: "Scarlet fever occurs most often in the winter and spring. Symptoms include a rash, a sore throat, flushed cheeks and swollen tongue.  Scarlet fever is caused by Streptococcus pyogenes or group A streptococci (GAS) bacteria. Occasionally these bacteria can cause severe and life-threatening diseases.  Scarlet fever is a notifiable disease in England and Wales. This means health professionals must inform local health protection teams of suspected cases.  "
